one_time_description: Comprehensive Settlement Agreement (CSA) regarding 2011 flooding - $20,400,000 trust fund recorded in 2020-21; in 2022 portfolio investments of $15,247,544 established from trust funds
economic_notes: Band operates Arena and Lottery, Grocery Store and Gas Bar, Motel and Entertainment Centre, Powder Horn Creek Restaurant and Gas Bar, and a Daycare Centre. Portfolio investments of $15,247,544 held in mutual funds with RBC Royal Trust from flooding settlement trust. CMHC housing replacement reserve underfunded by $478,641.
notes: Fiscal year 2022 (ended March 31, 2022). Prior year (2021) revenue was unusually high ($61.8M) due to $20.4M flooding settlement trust fund and $32.5M in federal capital transfers related to the same CSA. Nine fully consolidated subsidiaries including housing authority, health authority, grocery store, gas bar, motel, restaurant, arena, lottery, and daycare. No schedule of remuneration provided. Auditor identified only as 'Chartered Professional Accountants Inc.' based in Winnipeg - firm name not fully specified. Expenses_salaries_total from expenses by object ($4,984,518 wages and benefits); segmented total shows $4,984,519. Economic development expense in segmented note shows $671,021 actual vs $669,304 in statement of operations - minor rounding difference. Public works expense in segmented note shows $1,117,955 vs $926,704 in statement of operations due to segment including items differently. Statement of operations figures used for program expenses.
